emergent_themes.description: Delivery becomes integrated into highly structured work routines, creating predictable patterns that feel necessary rather than indulgent
Living alone and working independently increases delivery reliance as a substitute for social food decisions
Actively avoiding stressful food preparation after work, prioritizing calm and ease over other factors
Conscious recognition of delivery shifting from special occasions to routine behavior over time

coding_summary: P035 demonstrates a highly structured approach to delivery that has evolved from university social eating to independent work-life routines. Key patterns include predictable delivery triggers (work-from-home days, Fridays), stress avoidance prioritization, and increased reliance due to social isolation. She shows strong self-awareness about habit formation and value consciousness, with clear preferences for simplicity and efficiency in app interactions. Her future projections suggest delivery habits may decrease when returning to social university life but could increase again post-graduation depending on location and routine.
